session.title: Trace Coach: NodeRoom codebase trace
session.summary: A NodeRoom-looking local Trace tab seeded from NodeRoom's real trace UI source path: record list, detail tabs, steps, flow, screenshot boxes, and raw JSON.

surfaces.label: Trace tab
surfaces.about: NodeRoom's master-detail trace tab with Overview, Steps, Flow, Evidence, and Raw JSON.

surfaces.label: Room trace strip
surfaces.about: The bottom room activity strip that opens into the full trace surface.

surfaces.label: Trace steps
surfaces.about: Grouped, bounded, source-linked steps with screenshot and metric attachments.

surfaces.label: Trace flow
surfaces.about: NodeRoom's graph view over the same trace steps.

surfaces.label: Trace data
surfaces.about: The shared TraceRecord and TraceStep data model behind the tabs.

surfaces.label: Trace styling
surfaces.about: The NodeRoom visual layer for record lists, tabs, step cards, and screenshot boxes.

proofs.title: Step 01: Open NodeRoom's Trace tab from the artifact shell
proofs.detail: Start from the real artifact panel. NodeRoom exposes a trace tab and a bottom room trace strip, so users can move from live room activity into the full provenance view.

proofs.title: Step 02: Use the real NodeRoom trace tabs
proofs.detail: The sample should follow NodeRoom's detail-tab model instead of a loose walkthrough card: Overview, Steps, Flow, Evidence, and Raw JSON all hang off one selected trace record.

proofs.title: Step 03: Seed the app from NodeRoom TraceRecord data
proofs.detail: NodeTrace now seeds from the same mental model NodeRoom uses: records contain ordered steps, source metadata, verdicts, attachments, and raw audit payloads.

proofs.title: Step 04: Render code and UI evidence like NodeRoom step rows
proofs.detail: Each step is an ordered, source-linked unit. Screenshot attachments carry a normalized box so the UI can draw exactly what was clicked or extracted.

proofs.title: Step 05: Keep the latest flow tab behavior
proofs.detail: NodeRoom's Flow tab derives graph nodes and edges from the same ordered steps. Clicking a node opens the same step renderer, avoiding drift between graph and list.

proofs.title: Step 06: Match NodeRoom's dark trace surface
proofs.detail: The sample app should look like NodeRoom: dark app chrome, compact tabs, muted metadata, accent verdict pills, step cards, and screenshot highlight boxes.

traces.summary: Step 01: Open NodeRoom's Trace tab from the artifact shell
Step 02: Use the real NodeRoom trace tabs
Step 03: Seed the app from NodeRoom TraceRecord data
Step 04: Render code and UI evidence like NodeRoom step rows
Step 05: Keep the latest flow tab behavior
Step 06: Match NodeRoom's dark trace surface

steps.title: Open NodeRoom's Trace tab from the artifact shell
steps.narrative: Start from the real artifact panel. NodeRoom exposes a trace tab and a bottom room trace strip, so users can move from live room activity into the full provenance view.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom bottom room trace strip with recent room events and trace telemetry.

diagram.source:
flowchart LR
  Artifact[Artifact shell] --> Strip[Room trace strip]
  Strip --> TraceTab[Trace tab]
  TraceTab --> Surface[TraceSurface]

steps.title: Use the real NodeRoom trace tabs
steps.narrative: The sample should follow NodeRoom's detail-tab model instead of a loose walkthrough card: Overview, Steps, Flow, Evidence, and Raw JSON all hang off one selected trace record.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom trace detail tabs for Overview, Steps, Flow, Evidence, and Raw JSON.

diagram.source:
flowchart LR
  Record[Selected trace record] --> Overview
  Record --> Steps
  Record --> Flow
  Record --> Evidence
  Record --> RawJSON[Raw JSON]

steps.title: Seed the app from NodeRoom TraceRecord data
steps.narrative: NodeTrace now seeds from the same mental model NodeRoom uses: records contain ordered steps, source metadata, verdicts, attachments, and raw audit payloads.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom trace record list showing source, step count, and verdict pills.

diagram.source:
flowchart TB
  TraceRecord --> Source
  TraceRecord --> Verdict
  TraceRecord --> Steps
  Steps --> Attachments
  Attachments --> ScreenshotBox[normalized screenshot box]

steps.title: Render code and UI evidence like NodeRoom step rows
steps.narrative: Each step is an ordered, source-linked unit. Screenshot attachments carry a normalized box so the UI can draw exactly what was clicked or extracted.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom trace step screenshot frame with a highlighted bounding box overlay.

diagram.source:
flowchart LR
  Step[TraceStep] --> Label
  Step --> Detail
  Step --> Attachments
  Attachments --> Screenshot
  Screenshot --> Box[box x y w h]

codeBlock.snippet:
{s.label} {ssims.map((a, i) => 0.02)}>Δ {(a.diffRatio * 100).toFixed(1)}%)} {s.targetArtifactId && } {s.detail && {s.detail}} {s.screenshotUrl && ( )} {shots.map((a, i) => ( {a.box && } ))} {logs.map((a, i) => 
{a.text}
)} {s.metrics && ( {s.metrics.map((m) => {m.value} {m.label})} )} );

steps.title: Keep the latest flow tab behavior
steps.narrative: NodeRoom's Flow tab derives graph nodes and edges from the same ordered steps. Clicking a node opens the same step renderer, avoiding drift between graph and list.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom trace flow graph with grouped phases, animated edges, controls, and minimap.

diagram.source:
flowchart LR
  Steps[Ordered TraceStep list] --> Nodes[ReactFlow nodes]
  Nodes --> Edges[Sequential edges]
  Nodes --> Detail[Shared StepRow detail]

steps.title: Match NodeRoom's dark trace surface
steps.narrative: The sample app should look like NodeRoom: dark app chrome, compact tabs, muted metadata, accent verdict pills, step cards, and screenshot highlight boxes.

uiCapture.alt: NodeRoom dark trace surface with record list, detail pane, tabs, and step cards.

diagram.source:
flowchart TB
  Tokens[NodeRoom design tokens] --> TraceList
  Tokens --> DetailTabs
  Tokens --> StepCards
  Tokens --> ScreenshotBoxes

graphNodes.summary: Step renderer for source-linked rows and screenshot boxes.
